From Prestwich to the city centre, it's a new chapter for Dani Heron and Sofie Stoermann-Naess Go to Manchester's Finest X profile OSMA is set to return to Manchester this autumn, with founders Danielle Heron and Sofie Stoermann-Naess bringing the restaurant back in a new city centre location alongside an ambitious new grocery concept The restaurant will reopen at One Port Street in the Northern Quarter, almost two years after closing its original Prestwich site. The move marks a new chapter for the pair, who will also launch Boutikk, a neighbouring provisions store inspired by Scandinavian food culture and tr Originally opened in 2020, OSMA quickly established itself as one of Greater Manchester’s most acclaimed neighbourhood restaurants. Named after a combination of Oslo and Manchester, the restaurant blended Stoermann-Naess’ Norwegian heritage with Heron’s classical culinary trainin The return will see Heron resume her role as Executive Chef, with a menu centred around seasonal produce, fresh seafood, local meats and vegetable-led dishes. A number of OSMA’s most popular plates are expected to make a comeback, alongside the restaurant’s well-known sharing Sun While the restaurant occupies one side of the development, Boutikk will introduce a new offering to the city centre. The grocery and provisions shop will stock seasonal fruit and vegetables, dairy products, fresh meat and fish, with chefs available to advise customers on ingredie

Just four months after announcing plans to close, the Urmston restaurant is relaunching with a new sharing plates concept Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Back in February, it looked like the end for Örme The Michelin Guide-listed neighbourhood restaurant in Urmston announced plans to sell the business and close its doors, citing the ‘significant economic pressures’ facing the hospitality industry and describing long-term sustainability as ‘unfeasible’ Now, in a surprise turnaround, owners have confirmed it will remain open and relaunch on 1 July with a completely new concept centred around sharing plates, social dining and a more relaxed approach to eating out The restaurant, which opened in 2023 and quickly earned recognition from both the Michelin Guide and Good Food Guide, is moving away from its tasting menu format in favour of a regularly changing menu of snacks, small plates and desserts designed to be shared around the table Dishes set to feature on the opening menu include salt’n’vinegar scampi with curry mayo, smoked Lancashire cheese custard on fried bread with red onion jam, cured Cornish mackerel with roasted onion dressing and English peas, and spring lamb served with sweet and sour mint and BB

Since they started in 2020, Eat Well MCR is a lifeline to people across the region By Manchester's Finest, Last updated 17 June 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Since it started in the days after lockdown, Eat Well MCR has delivered more than 150,000 meals to people who need them across Greater Manchester – all cooked by some of the city’s most prestigious restaurants Recruiting the like of Dishoom , Hawksmoor , Hello Oriental and Maray , among many, many others (too many to name check here), they give back to the city without fanfare and for all the right reasons We spent a day out on the road with some of the team, to see first-hand some of the vital work they do – from the kitchens making up the meals all the way through to the people who receive them The whole thing is magnificent; a testament both to the tenacity of its founders, Kathleen O’Connor, Gemma Saunders and Mary-Ellen McTague, and also to the city’s chefs who quietly but diligently support what has become an essential lifeline for so many

Aspire Pre‑Flight Hospitality (Aspire), Swissport International’s airport hospitality brand, has announced the opening of a new lounge at Manchester Airport Following continued growth in the UK&I market, including new lounges in Liverpool and Birmingham, this latest expansion highlights increasing demand for high-quality pre-flight hospitality Opening in late August 2026, the new lounge is centrally located in the terminal, away from the hustle and bustle of the busy departures area. Featuring 300 seats and designed for all travellers, the lounge combines award-winning service, thoughtfully designed surroundings, and e “We’re delighted to announce our third lounge at Manchester Airport and second in Terminal 2. With a capacity of over 300, our guests can be assured of world-class, award-winning hospitality and a lounge designed to reflect our almost 40 years of experience in partnership with th The lounge has been designed with all types of passengers in mind, with spaces for small groups and families to socialise together, places to dine as well as a quiet area and private work rooms for those needing a sanctuary to catch up on emails. The decoration and design evoke a “We’re pleased to announce this latest opening at Manchester Airport as we continue to build out our lounge portfolio with new and expanded presence across key airports and markets worldwide. This Aspire lounge brings the very best in lounge design and all the amenities that toda

You can grab both hot and cold pies to take away At long last, Patel’s Pies – the proper pie shop with Indian-inspired fillings – has got its very own site, and we couldn’t be happier about it The business is already a familiar name (to the blue half of the city, of course) thanks to its matchday spot outside the Etihad Stadium , but now, Patel’s Pies will be serving its delicious savoury bakes to the people of the Heatons too – with its brand-new pie shop now opene at Here you’ll find their legendary curry pies with homemade pastry – filled with the likes of keema, butter chicken, and beef madras You can grab hot and cold pies to take away, all setting you back an affordable £5.95 each From 11am-2pm, you can also get your hands on the lunch deal, which is any pie, masala mash, spiced mushy peas, and gunpowder gravy

A pizzeria that started life in Manchester has been named one of the world’s best in a prestigious global ranking Forbici, the restaurant where all pizzas are served with a pair of scissors, is the only northern pizzeria to be recognised at yesterday’s Best Pizza Awards 2026 The awards took place in Milan last night, and are said to be the Michelin equivalent of the pizza world Forbici was named as creating one of the world’s best pizzas, with its master chef recognised as one of the top pizza chefs in the world It’s one of just two UK pizzerias listed in the coveted top 100 this year Forbici opened on the corner of Cross Street back in 2024, and has since expanded across to Sheffield, with a Trafford Centre restaurant set to open soon too

A tiny new coffee shop has opened in Ancoats, and it’s already turning heads with a simple mission – making quality coffee affordable again 7ZZ (pronounced ‘seven zeez’) has quietly launched on Oldham Road in a space no bigger than your arm span Owner Joe Stephens is bringing speciality coffee prices back down to earth with flat whites starting from just £2.50 and matcha from only £4 In a city where a morning coffee can easily set you back more than a fiver, the new independent hopes to bridge the gap between premium coffee shops and budget chains The compact café may be easy to miss at first glance, but its menu packs plenty of personality. Alongside classic espresso-based drinks, customers can pick up ceremonial-grade matcha, iced barista-made coffees, and vibrant ube lattes made properly (no purple syrup in sight) Joe says the idea behind 7ZZ was to create a space where customers don’t have to choose between quality and affordability, with a price list that rivals Greggs

A cocktail bar in Manchester has received a surprise listing in the prestigious Michelin Guide – the first drinks-led venue in the city to gain such recognition Posie opened less than a year ago in an unlikely location just off Market Street, transforming a former sandwich shop into a sunny and stylish bar, right in the heart of Manchester city centre The bar comes from the same team behind 10 Tib Lane (which is also a Michelin Guide venue) and is famed for its cocktails, oysters, and global wine list And now Posie has swooped into the prestigious guide just 10 months after its launch, with judges celebrating its small plates and its extensive cocktail list The Michelin Guide said: “Founded by the same team as nearby 10 Tib Lane, posie is a little more drink-driven than its sibling, with a global wine selection that’s keen on natural options, alongside over 30 classic and bespoke cocktails and mocktails “None of this means that they don’t take their food seriously, with plenty of flavour bursting out of the concise small-plates selection

We may be halfway through the year already, but the second half of 2026 is shaping up nicely with some huge new openings still to come There are reborn pubs, glitzy new restaurants, giant street food venues, and even a bookshop/wine bar hybrid On the list of new openings coming to Greater Manchester this year, you’ll spot a fair few familiar names from the region, some total newcomers, and some big businesses from elsewhere in the UK And these new openings span everywhere from Stockport to the city centre, and from the Etihad Campus to… well, a secret location we actually don’t know yet Here are a dozen things to look forward to this year Could you imagine a better afternoon than buying a new book and then reading the first few chapters with a glass of wine? Me neither, which is why you’re going to LOVE this Stockport newcomer

Stockport’s Asian Food Night Market returns every second Friday of the month The sun is shining and so are the flavours! Whether you're after some delicious food or just soaking in the atmosphere, July's night market is the place to be. Come hungry, leave happy Come for the food. Stay for the atmosphere. Bring your friends :)

The Makers Market takes over Market Place with around 70 independent traders selling handmade goods, artisan food, and independent finds against the backdrop of Stockport's historic market town architecture Think craft, ceramics, prints, jewellery, candles, street food, and of unique gifts and creations you won't find anywhere else 11th July, 8th August, 12th September, 10th October, 14th November, 12th December The nearest bus stop is Stockport Interchange (around a 12-minute walk). Cycle racks are available on-site

The family-friendly event will be packed with all kinds of zines, prints and artwork for sale from over 40 traders New to zines? A free, all-ages workshop will be running all day where you can make your own zine to photocopy and trade. Perfect for building (or starting!) your collection Feeling creative? Join in making badges or contribute to a new collaborative zine that will be created throughout the day

Stockport’s Asian Food Night Market returns every second Friday of the month The sun is shining and so are the flavours! Whether you're after some delicious food or just soaking in the atmosphere, June's night market is the place to be. Come hungry, leave happy Come for the food. Stay for the atmosphere. Bring your friends :)

The Manchester restaurant is opening up its mezzanine kitchen to a new independent food concept Eating & Drinking ⸱ Food Halls and Markets ⸱ Street Food Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Hello Oriental is searching for a new East Asian food operator to take over the mezzanine level at its Circle Square venue The restaurant, bar and events space, located just off Oxford Road, is looking for an independent chef, pop-up, street food trader or established brand to join the site as it develops what the team believes will be Manchester’s first multi-vendor destination dedicated to East Asi Unlike a traditional food hall, the plan is to keep the focus on East Asian cooking, with Hello Oriental looking for a concept that complements its existing food and drink offer The team is particularly interested in ramen, noodles, dumplings, Korean street food, Japanese comfort food, Taiwanese dishes, Hong Kong-style food, barbecue, izakaya-style small plates or modern East Asian fusion

Porta and Tartuffe have spent months building a 10 course Spanish-inspired menu of Gildas, grilled seafood, chicken thighs and giant open-fire paella Go to Manchester's Finest X profile There are few restaurants that inspire the kind of loyalty Porta does. The sort of place where you go in for a vermouth and a couple of plates, then realise two hours later that you’ve ordered half the menu and are seriously considering another That easy-going, no-fuss approach has made the walk-in-only tapas bar a favourite across its four sites in Chester, Altrincham, Salford and West Didsbury. Founded by brothers Ben and Joe Wright, Porta takes its cues from the neighbourhood bars of Spain: short menus, good produce, This July, Porta is leaving the comfort of its own dining rooms to join resident chefs Tartuffe at Torr Vale Mill for the next Open Fire Dining Club . It’s a collaboration that has been in the works for months Tartuffe’s Gary Weir and Porta’s executive chef José Garzón have spent that time trading ideas and finding the right meeting point between the two kitchens. José brings an extensive knowledge of Spanish cooking, while Gary has his own history with the country after spending time There are recognisable signatures from both sides. Porta’s much-loved grilled chicken thighs make an appearance with mojo picón and smoked potato. Tartuffe’s Gilda, a regular on its own menu, has been reworked as a gilda de pulpo with Gordal olive, pickled chilli and braised octo

Danish-born juice bar and coffee concept JOE & THE JUICE is bringing its signature juices, coffee, and cult-favourite sandwiches to Manchester this summer with the launch of Sunlight House The opening of Sunlight House marks a major expansion for the juice bar in Manchester, continuing its growth across the city following the launch of its Mosley Street store last month and ahead of a new Oxford Road opening later this summer To celebrate launch day on Friday 10th July at, JOE & THE JUICE will be treating the first 100 customers through the doors to a free juice Designed with JOE & THE JUICE’s modern Scandinavian heritage in mind, the store will feature the brand’s instantly recognisable open counter layout, minimalist interiors and natural materials. The space will also integrate the JOE app experience, allowing customers to pre-order, The Sunlight House menu will showcase JOE’s full range of freshly prepared, functional offerings. Guests can enjoy its iconic juices and wellness shots, alongside protein shakes, specialty coffee and matcha drinks, as well as its popular Scandi-style sandwiches made fresh using t JOE & THE JUICE Sunlight House will open on Friday 10th July 2026, from 9am

Gaucho Manchester begins an exciting new chapter as the restaurant unveils its newly redesigned interior in the heart of the city Marking more than 25 years in Manchester, thereopening celebrates the next evolution of the brand in a city that has been central to its story The newly transformed 205-cover space introduces a warm, contemporary design direction inspired by the landscapes, textures and spirit of Latin America. A timeless palette of earthy tones is layered with natural timber, rich marble, atmospheric lighting and refined finishes,creat Housed within one of Manchester’s most distinctive 19th-century former churches, the new design complements the original character of the building Restored architectural features,including the original church organ, remain central to the space while a mezzanine dining level offers elevated views across the restaurant below At the centre of the restaurant, a renewed open kitchen brings the theatre of the grill into view, creating a closer connection between guests and the craft behind every dish
